description: Head-receipt verification MUST fail when the receipt's head fields do not byte-match the accompanying /current response (RFC-ACDP-0011 §7 step 5). Here the registry serves version 2 as the current head but attaches a (genuinely signed, previously valid) receipt still attesting version 1 — the stale-head-serving scenario head receipts exist to make attributable. The receipt's own signature verifies; the failure is the head binding, not the cryptography.

input.scenario: Consumer calls GET /lineages/lin:sha256:c7fef01c…/current. The response body is version 2 (head_ctx_id acdp://registry.example.com/aaaaaaaa-…), registry_state.status 'active', but lineage_head_receipt is the lhr-001 golden receipt attesting version 1.

expected.behavior: Steps 1–4 of RFC-ACDP-0011 §7 pass (closed parse, signature verifies under the registry key, registry binding holds, lineage_id matches). Step 5 fails: on /current the receipt MUST describe the very head being served — head_ctx_id 'acdp://…/12345678-…' ≠ body.ctx_id 'acdp://…/aaaaaaaa-…' and head_version 1 ≠ body.version 2. The head receipt MUST be reported as failed (invalid_receipt category). The converse reading is the detection value: if the registry instead serves the v1 body WITH this receipt while a fresher receipt (or another vantage) shows a v2 head, the two signed receipts are the registry's own conflicting evidence of stale-head serving (RFC-ACDP-0011 §11).
expected.verdict_independence: The served body's producer signature and content_hash, and any RFC-ACDP-0010 registry_receipt on the response, are verified independently and may still pass — only the serve-time head attestation is invalid (RFC-ACDP-0011 §7).
